title: My Aunt & Uncle Sold Mom's $310K Stamps For RV, Lied It Flooded. So I Made His Name The Donor.
description: When my aunt and uncle sold my late mother's rare stamp collection for $310K and lied it was lost in a flood, I didn't just walk away. I traced the stamps to a Zurich collector, sued for conversion, and repossessed the entire collection. I then sold the stamps at a premium to create a women's entrepreneurship grant, with each certificate permanently embossed with my uncle's name as the unwilling donor. #Betrayal #RareStamps #JusticeServed
thumb_text: EVERY GRANT BEARS HIS NAME AS UNWILLING DONOR
pinned_comment: If your aunt and uncle sold your $310K inheritance and lied it was lost in a flood, would you have taken them to court to reclaim it, or would you have let it go? Share your story in the comments.

thumb_lines.text: I TRACED THE SALE TO A ZURICH COLLECTOR AND SUED
